meta.title: Work, Gravitational Potential Energy & Kinetic Energy
meta.subject: Physics · Energy

sets.q: A force of 40 N is applied to a box, moving it 5 m in the direction of the force. How much work is done?
sets.given:
Formula: W = F × d × cos θ
Force and displacement are in the same direction, so θ = 0°, cos 0° = 1.

sets.q: A 60 N force pushes a crate 3 m in the direction of the force. How much work is done?
A 25 N force drags a sled 8 m horizontally in the direction of force. How much work is done?
sets.given: W = F × d × cos 0° = F × d

sets.q: A 15 N force acts on a box over a displacement of 10 m in the same direction. Calculate the work done.
A 50 N force is applied over 4 m in the direction of motion. What is the work done?
A force of 80 N is applied at 60° to the horizontal, moving an object 6 m horizontally. How much work is done?
sets.given:
W = F × d × cos θ
cos 60° = 0.5

sets.q: A 50 N force at 60° to the horizontal moves a box 4 m. Calculate the work done.
A 100 N force at 60° moves a crate 3 m horizontally. How much work is done?
A 40 N force is applied at 60° to the horizontal over a displacement of 5 m. Find the work done.
A 60 N force applied at 60° moves an object 10 m. What work is done on the object?
A 5 kg book is lifted to a shelf 2 m high. How much gravitational potential energy does it gain? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 3 kg object is raised 4 m. What is its gravitational potential energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 10 kg box is lifted 3 m. Find the gravitational potential energy stored. (g = 10 m/s²)
A 2 kg ball is thrown up to a height of 5 m. What is its GPE at the highest point? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 7 kg rock rests on a ledge 6 m above the ground. What is its gravitational potential energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 4 kg object moves at 5 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
A 6 kg ball rolls at 4 m/s. Calculate its kinetic energy.
A 2 kg stone moves at 10 m/s. What is its kinetic energy?
A 10 kg cart moves at 3 m/s. Find its kinetic energy.
A 8 kg bicycle moves at 5 m/s. What is the kinetic energy?
A 3 kg ball falls from a height of 5 m. What is its speed just before hitting the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given:
GPE converts to KE: mgh = ½mv²
Solve for v: v = √(2gh)

sets.q: A 2 kg object is dropped from 20 m. What is its speed just before impact? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 5 kg rock falls from 8 m. Find its speed just before landing. (g = 10 m/s²)
A 1 kg ball drops from a height of 45 m. What is its speed just before hitting the ground? (g = 10 m/s²)
A stone is dropped from 20 m. What is its kinetic energy just before it hits the ground? (mass = 2 kg, g = 10 m/s²)
A 500 N force is applied perpendicular to a wall that does not move. How much work is done?
sets.given:
W = F × d × cos θ
No displacement occurs → d = 0.

sets.q: You push with all your might against a fixed boulder but it doesn't move. How much work have you done?
sets.given: Work requires displacement in the direction of force.

sets.q: A force is applied perpendicular to the direction of motion. How much work does this force do?
sets.given: W = F × d × cos θ. If θ = 90°, cos 90° = 0.

sets.q: A satellite moves in a circular orbit at constant speed. How much work does gravity do over one complete orbit?
sets.given: For a circular orbit, gravity is always perpendicular to the velocity.
opts: 0 J — gravity is always perpendicular to motion

sets.q: A 200 N weight is carried horizontally for 10 m at constant height. How much work is done against gravity?
sets.given: Gravity acts downward; displacement is horizontal. θ = 90° between them.

sets.q: A 5 kg object has 250 J of kinetic energy. What is its speed?
sets.given:
Rearrange KE = ½mv²:
v = √(2 × KE / m)

sets.q: A 2 kg ball has 100 J of kinetic energy. What is its speed?
A 8 kg cart has 400 J of kinetic energy. What is its speed?
A 10 kg object has 180 J of kinetic energy. What is its speed?
A 4 kg object has 72 J of kinetic energy. What is its speed?
If speed doubles, what happens to an object's kinetic energy?
sets.given: KE = ½mv². Substitute v → 2v and compare.

sets.q: If speed is tripled, kinetic energy becomes:
Speed is halved. What happens to kinetic energy?
Mass doubles while speed stays constant. What happens to kinetic energy?
Both mass and speed double. What is the new kinetic energy relative to the original?
sets.given: KE = ½mv². New m = 2m, new v = 2v.

sets.q: A 10 kg roller coaster car starts from rest at a height of 20 m. What is its maximum speed at ground level? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 5 kg pendulum bob is released from a height of 0.8 m. What is its maximum speed at the lowest point? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 3 kg ball is released from rest at a height of 5 m on a frictionless ramp. What is its speed at the bottom? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 2 kg object slides down a frictionless slope of height 45 m. What is its speed at the bottom? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 1 kg marble rolls from a height of 3.2 m down a frictionless incline. What is its speed at the bottom? (g = 10 m/s²)
A 4 kg object is at rest at the top of a 5 m ramp. What is its total mechanical energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given:
Total Mechanical Energy = KE + GPE
At rest: KE = 0. Total = GPE = mgh.

sets.q: A 3 kg object moves at 4 m/s at the ground (h = 0). What is its total mechanical energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: At ground level: GPE = 0. Total = KE = ½mv².

sets.q: A 5 kg object at a height of 4 m moves at 6 m/s. What is the total mechanical energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: Total ME = KE + GPE = ½mv² + mgh

sets.q: A 2 kg ball at rest is held at h = 10 m. What is its mechanical energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: ME = KE + GPE. At rest, KE = 0.

sets.q: A 6 kg object is at h = 0 m and moves at 8 m/s. What is its mechanical energy? (g = 10 m/s²)
sets.given: ME = KE + GPE. At ground, GPE = 0.
